California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 25, 1989

William Stewart
President of Board
Pico Water District
4843 S. Church Street
P.O. Box 758
Pico Rivera, CA  90660

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. G-89-588

Dear Mr. Stewart:

     By letter dated October 2, 1989, you requested assistance
from the California Attorney General's office concerning the
eligibility of Manuel Higuera Jurado to serve as an elected of-
ficial. You provided us with a copy of that letter and attached
documents, which we received on October 10, 1989.

     A review of these materials indicates that your request does
not present a question under the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹  Therefore, since our jurisdiction extends only to mat-
ters covered by the Act, we cannot provide you with assistance
regarding this matter.

     If you have any questions concerning the Act, please contact
me at (916) 322-5901.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Kathryn E. Donovan
                                   General Counsel

                                   By: John W. Wallace
                                       Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JWW:plh


¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

Dear Sir:

Received the attached information on a fellow Board Member. Have been
asked by other Board Members to inquire as to this person's eligibility
to serve as an elected official of State of California.

Respectively yours,

W.D. Stewart
President of Board
Pico Water District

cc: Los Angeles County
Regional Office of the District Attorney
19720 East Arrow Highway
Covina, CA 91722

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 17, 1989

William D. Stewart
President
Pico Water District
P.O. Box 758
Pico Rivera, CA  90660

Re:  Letter No. 89-588

Dear Mr. Stewart:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on October 10, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to what
information needed. If your request is for formal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[illegible]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

DEFENDANT IS THE SECOND OF TWO CHILDREN BORN

IN 1970. THEY HAVE ONE CHILD AGE 16 WHO RESIDES WITH DEFENDANT.

APPROXIMATELY THE LAST 13 YEARS. UP TO THE TIME OF ARREST ON
THE INSTANT MATTER, HE WAS WORKING AS A REAL ESTATE SALESMAN
FOR GEORGE REYES REALTY IN MONTEBELLO AND AT THE SAME TIME
AND IS STILL EMPLOYED BY DILSAVER REALTY, 610 NORTH TAYLOR
DRIVE, MONTEREY PARK. AT THE TIME OF THE INTERVIEW, DEFENDANT
STATED THAT HIS EMPLOYER DID NOT KNOW OF THE INSTANT MATTER AND
DEFENDANT INSISTED THAT HE HAS A CURRENT VALID REAL ESTATE
SALESMAN'S LICENSE. UNDERSIGNED TALKED WITH MR. DILSAVER WHO
STATED THAT HE IS AWARE OF DEFENDANT'S PRESENT CASE AND THAT
ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT WORKS FOR HIM IN SOME CAPACITIES, HE IS NOT
EMPLOYED IN ANY CAPACITY REQUIRING A LICENSE AS HE KNOWS THAT
DEFENDANT DOES NOT HAVE AN ACTIVE LICENSE (SEE INTERESTED PARTIES
SECTION).

DEFENDANT STATES HE HAS A YEARLY INCOME OF
APPROXIMATELY $12,000. HE STATES HE INHERITED HIS HOME FROM HIS
MOTHER AND THE HOME IS VALUED AT APPROXIMATELY $24,500. HE
STATES HE OWNS IT CLEAR EXCEPT FOR APPROXIMATELY $2,500 WHICH
HE BORROWED TO MEET THE COSTS OF PROBATE, ETC. HE OWNS A 1973
BUICK ON WHICH HE STILL OWES $800. HE STATES HE IS STILL
HOLDING ONE SECOND TRUST DEED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000 WHICH HE
IS INTENDING TO FORECLOSE.

DEFENDANT SERVED IN THE U.S. ARMY FROM 1945 TO
1946 AND WAS HONORABLY DISCHARGED. HE STATES HE SERVED ONLY
ONE YEAR AS THE ARMY WAS DEMOBILIZING AT THAT TIME. HE WAS ALSO
IN THE MERCHANT MARINES FROM 1944 TO 1945.

NARCOTICS AND INTOXICANTS:

DEFENDANT DENIES THE PAST OR PRESENT USE OF ANY

TYPE OF ILLEGAL DRUG OR NARCOTIC. HE STATES HE RARELY DRINKS
AND ONLY OH SOCIAL OCCASIONS. HIS ARREST RECORD WAS NOT
AVAILABLE AT THE TIME OF INTERVIEW.

ARREST RECORD:

SOURCES OF INFORMATION:

LAPD, LASO AND CII.

12-25-48        LAPO - SUSPICION ROBBERY; RELEASED 12-2-48.

2-14-51         LASO SUSPICION OF RAPE; NO DISPOSITION SHOWN.
1-26-52
                LASU - VIOLATION PROBATION - FELONY WARRANT
                AND DRUNK DRIVING; NO DISPOSITION SHOWN.

                (IN THE LOS ANGELES POLICE DEPARTMENT FILE ON MICROFILM,
                THERE IS A DISPOSITION CARD DATED NOVEMBER 19, 1952
                WHICH SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT RECEIVED 180 DAYS, 45 DAYS
                CREDIT, AND THREE YEARS PROBATION. IT IS UNSURE WHETHER
                THIS REFERS TO DRUNK DRIVING OR THE RAPE CHARGES.)

1-13-29         LAPD - WARRANT VIOLATION - ST. PROBATION.

                (A CHECK WAS MADE WITH COUNTY PROBATION AS TO THEIR
                EARLY FILES, HOWEVER, THEY HAVE BEEN DESTROYED.)

12-23-56        LAPD - 501 PC DRUNK DRIVING FELONY.

2-7-57          LAPD - 502 VC DRUNK DRIVING.

9-1-63          LASO - DRUNK DRIVING MISDEMEANOR.

2-8-67          LAPD - 23102 VC; NO DISPOSITION ON ANY OF THE
                ABOVE.

                (WHEN ASKED REGARDING PRIOR ARRESTS, DEFENDANT STATED
                THAT HE HAD HAD TWO DRUNK DRIVING ARRESTS IN 1956
                ON WHICH HE PAID FINES. HE DID NOT MENTION A PRIOR
                PROBATION CASE.)

                PRESENT OFFENSE:

                A COMPLAINT WAS FILED ALLEGING 487.1 PENAL CODE

                AND A WARRANT OF ARREST ISSUED AT THE REQUEST OF THE STATE

                DEPARTMENT OF REAL ESTATE. DEFENDANT WAS ARRESTED BY DISTRICT

                ATTORNEY INVESTIGATORS ON FEBRUARY 18, 1975, AT 4843 CHURCH

                STREET, PICO RIVERA. HE WAS SUBSEQUENTLY CHARGED ON THE

                -3-

[illegible]

INFORMATION WITH ONE COUNT 487.1 PENAL CODE--GRAND THEFT AND...

ON OCTOBER 21, 1971 PLEADED GUILTY TO A MISDEMEANOR. MATTER

WAS CONTINUED TO THIS DATE FOR A PROBATION AND SENTENCE HEARING.

THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE CASE AS CONTAINED IN THE DISTRICT

ATTORNEY'S FILE APPEAR TO BE AS FOLLOWS:

AS ADMINISTRATRIX OF HER DECEASED AUNT'S PROPERTY,

VICTIM IN FEBRUARY 1973, TOOK OVER A PRE-EXISTING ARRANGEMENT

WHEREBY DEFENDANT COLLECTED THE RENTS ON VICTIM'S PROPERTY,

TOOK TEN PER CENT FOR HIS SERVICES, AND SENT THE REMAINDER TO

VICTIM. THIS PROCEDURE WENT ALONG AS ARRANGED THROUGH JUNE

OF 1973. HOWEVER, FROM JULY 1973 THROUGH SEPTEMBER 1973 VICTIM

DID NOT RECEIVE ANY PAYMENT. DEFENDANT DID NOT ANSWER HER

LETTERS NOR RESPOND TO A PERSONAL VISIT TO DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYER

BY VICTIM'S HUSBAND. VICTIM WAS UNDER THE IMPRESSION THAT

DEFENDANT WAS A DULY LICENSED REAL ESTATE AGENT AND A COMPLAINT

WAS FILED WITH THE DEPARTMENT OF REAL ESTATE. THE DEPARTMENT

INVESTIGATOR OBTAINED RECEIPTS SHOWING THAT THE RENTS HAD BEEN

PAID TO DEFENDANT FOR THE MONTHS IN QUESTION. IN ADDITION,

SOME OF THE CHECKS RECEIVED WERE DEPOSITED IN DEFENDANT'S

PERSONAL COMMERCIAL ACCOUNT AT GOLDEN STATE BANK IN PICO RIVERA

AND USED IN PAYMENT FOR OTHER MATTERS.

DEFENDANT'S STATEMENT:

NO WRITTEN STATEMENT HAS BEEN RECEIVED FROM

DEFENDANT. ORALLY, DEFENDANT STATES THAT THE MATTER WAS A

MISUNDERSTANDING AND INVOLVED A SECRETARY WHO IS NO LONGER WITH

HIM. HE STATED THAT HE DID COLLECT THE MONEY, SOME IN CASH

AND SOME IN CHECKS AND TURNED IT OVER TO HIS SECRETARY AND IT

SEEMED THAT SHE HAD SENT IT ON TO THE VICTIM. IT WAS POINTED-

OUT TO HIM THAT SOME OF THE CHECKS HAD WOUND UP IN HIS BANK.

ACCOUNT, AND HE THEN STATED THAT HE FREQUENTLY DEPOSITED CHECKS

IN HIS ACCOUNT, USED THE MONEY TO MAKE PAYMENT ON IMMEDIATE

BILLS AND THEN WOULD SEND A CHECK OF HIS OWN TO THE OWNER OF

THE PROPERTY. HE STATED HE DID NOT KNOW THAT CO-MINGLING OF

FUNDS WAS A CRIMINAL OFFENSE. DEFENDANT STATES HE CANNOT ACCOUNT

FOR SOME OF THE CASH MONEY THAT HE TURNED OVER TO HIS SECRETARY.

SINCE THE MATTER WAS BROUGHT TO HIS ATTENTION, HE STATES HE

HAS PAID FULL RESTITUTION TO THE OWNERS OF THE PROPERTY.

INTERESTED PARTIES:

DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY VERIFIES THAT HE SENT A

CHECK FOR $1,625 TO VICTIM'S ATTORNEY ON OCTOBER 27, 1975.

$1,407 WAS FOR THE COLLECTED RENTS, $197.06 IN INTEREST AND

AN ADDITIONAL $20.00 FOR COSTS. THIS WAS IN RESPONSE TO A CIVIL

COURT ACTION. RECEIPT OF THE CHECK WAS VERIFIED WITH THE

OFFICE OF VICTIM'S ATTORNEY, LENARD ZIPPERIAN, (916) 441-0127,

WHO REPRESENTS RILLA FALL, 6412 PARK CREST WAY, CITRUS HEIGHTS,

CALIFORNIA. IT WAS VERIFIED THAT THE OFFICE HAD RECEIVED

THE CHECK WHICH WAS PAYMENT IN FULL AND THAT THE CIVIL ACTION

HAD BEEN DISMISSED.

MR. BOB SCHOFIELD OF THE STATE DEPARTMENT OF

REAL ESTATE, (650-2724) STATES DEFENDANT'S CASE IS NOT BEING

COVERED BY AN INVESTIGATOR AT THE CURRENT TIME PENDING THE

OUTCOME OF THE CRIMINAL MATTER. HIS FILE NUMBER H-18222 LA

SHOWS THAT HIS LICENSE WAS SUSPENDED INDEFINITELY ON JULY 14,

1974. MR. SCHOFIELD CHECKED WITH SACRAMENTO RECORDS WHICH SHOWED

NO REINSTATEMENT OF THE LICENSE. HE STATES THE ONLY ACTIVITY

-5-

1    IN WHICH DEFENDANT CAN ENGAGE IS BUYING A HOUSE ON HIS OWN

2    OR SELLING A HOUSE ON HIS OWN.  DEFENDANT HAD A PRIOR REVOCATION

3    OF HIS LICENSE IN AUGUST OF 1963 UNDER CASE NUMBER H-17678 LA

4    FOR "ACTING DISHONESTLY" IN RELATION TO BORROWING MONEY ON AN

5    UNRECORDED SECOND TRUST DEED.  DEFENDANT'S LICENSE WAS SUSPENDED

6    FOR 60 DAYS AND THEREAFTER HE WAS ISSUED ONLY A RESTRICTED LICENSE

7    WHICH HAS BEEN IN EFFECT UNTIL THE ABOVE SUSPENSION OF JULY 14,

8    1974.  THERE WERE SEVERAL REQUESTS BY DEFENDANT TO GET HIS FULL

9    LICENSE REINSTATED BUT THE REQUESTS WERE DENIED AS DEFENDANT

10   HAD NOT COMPLIED WITH REQUIREMENTS SUCH AS FILING A MONTHLY

11   REPORT WITH THE DEPARTMENT.

12   EVALUATION:

13   DEFENDANT HAS MADE FULL RESTITUTION TO VICTIM

14   ON THE INSTANT MATTER.  HIS ACTIVITIES IN RELATION TO REAL

15   ESTATE WILL CONTINUE TO BE MONITORED BY THE STATE DEPARTMENT OF

16   REAL ESTATE.  IN VIEW OF THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT HAS HAD A

17   SIMILAR TYPE OF OFFENSE IN THE PAST, IT IS FELT THAT A PERIOD OF

18   FORMAL PROBATION WOULD BE APPROPRIATE AND THAT A MODERATE FINE

19   IN LIEU OF TIME IN CUSTODY WOULD BE SUFFICIENT.

20

21   RECOMMENDATION:

22   IT IS RECOMMENDED THAT PROBATION BE GRANTED

23   UNDER THE FOLLOWING TERMS AND CONDITIONS:

24   2.  PAY A FINE, PLUS PENALTY ASSESSMENT, TO
        THE PROBATION OFFICER IN SUCH MANNER AS
25        HE SHALL PRESCRIBE.

26   14. SEEK AND MAINTAIN TRAINING, SCHOOLING OR

27        EMPLOYMENT AS APPROVED BY THE PROBATION
        OFFICER.

28   15. MAINTAIN RESIDENCE AS APPROVED BY THE
        PROBATION OFFICER.

29

    -6-

20. OBEY ALL LAWS, ORDERS, RULES AND
REGULATIONS OF THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT
AND OF THE COURT.

NOT ENGAGE IN REAL ESTATE SALES ACTIVITY
WITHOUT BEING PROPERLY LICENSED.

RESPECTFULLY SUBMITTED,

CLARENCE E. CABELL,
ACTING PROBATION OFFICER

487.1 OI CT

NATURE OF PROCEEDINGS

TRIAL    JURY    OR

Jury deliberation continued from May 17, 1976, resumes with all
parties present as heretofore.

At 2125 p.m. the jury returns into Court in the presence of the
defendant and all counsel with the following verdict:

" TITLE OF COURT AND CAUSE "

" We the jury in the above-entitled action find the Defendant,
MANUEL JURADO, JR., guilty of Violating Section 487.1 of the
Penal Code, a felony as charged in Count 1 of the Information
and we further find it to be GRAND THEFT ( Over $200.00)."

This 18 day of May 1976 Joseph R. Cox Foreman. "

Upon request of the defendant the jury is polled as to the verdict
and all answers in the affirmative. The verdict is recorded.
Reading of the verdict as recorded is waived. Instructions
"Given" and "Refused" and all verdict forms are filed.

The jury is thanked and excused.

The defendant is referred to the Probation Department for
Probation and Sentencing set for June 28, 1976.

The defendant is allowed to remain on O.R.

The People request that People's Exhibit 3a, 3b, 3c, be returned
to their owner and photostatic copies of the checks be substituted
in the place of the originals. The Court so orders.

OR

     I, Eual J. Austin, a Deputy Real Estate Commissioner
of the State of California, do hereby certify that as of March
1974 Manuel Jurado, Jr. was licensed as a restricted renewal
real estate salesman in the employ of Elmo Herman Morales,
5319 East Beverly Boulevard, Suite B, Los Angeles 90022. This
license was canceled March 23, 1974. This license was to be
in effect through April 19, 1977, but was suspended indefinite
by Order of the Real Estate Commissioner effective July 14,
1974.

     The last residence address of record as shown on a
real estate salesman application for transfer filed with this
Department on November 20, 1973 by Manuel Jurado, Jr. is 4411
South DeLand Avenue, Pico Rivera 90660.

     Witness my hand and the seal of the Real Estate
Commissioner of the State of California this 23rd day of
November, 1976.


                                    _______________________
                                    Deputy Real Estate Commissioner
                                    of the State of California

embedding.qa_text: **California Fair Political Practices Commission** October 25, 1989 William Stewart President of Board Pico Water District 4843 S. Church Street P.O. Box 758 Pico Rivera, CA 90660 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. G-89-588 Dear Mr. Stewart: By letter dated October 2, 1989, you requested assistance from the California Attorney General’s office concerning the eligibility of Manuel Higuera Jurado to serve as an elected official. You provided us with a copy of that letter and attached documents, which we received on October 10, 1989. A review of these materials indicates that your request does not present a question under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Therefore, since our jurisdiction extends only to matters covered by the Act, we cannot provide you with assistance regarding this matter. If you have any questions concerning the Act, please contact me at (916) 322-5901. Sincerely, Kathryn E. Donovan General Counsel [Signature] By: John W. Wallace Counsel, Legal Division KED:JWW:plh 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. October 2, 1989 Department of Justice State Attorney General - John K. Van De Kamp 1515 K Street Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Sir: Received the attached information on a fellow Board Member. Have been asked by other Board Members to inquire as to this person's eligibility to serve as an elected official of State of California. Respectively yours, W.D. Stewart President of Board Pico Water District cc: Los Angeles County Regional Office of the District Attorney 19720 East Arrow Highway Covina, CA 91722 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 "J" Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95814 October 17, 1989 William D. Stewart President Pico Water District P.O. Box 758 Pico Rivera, CA 90660 Re: Letter No. 89-588, Dear Mr. Stewart: Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on October 10, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to information needed. If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. Very truly yours, Kathryn E. Donovan General Counsel KED:plh This document is a Probation Officer's Report from the Superior Court of California, County of Los Angeles, dated June 23, 1976. The report is related to a case involving Manuel Higuera Jurado, who was charged with Grand Theft under Penal Code section 487.1. The report
